title: Pilosella × variifurca
intro: Pilosella × variifurca, also known as Alpine hawk's beard, is a hybrid plant species within the dandelion family that exhibits a unique combination of characteristics from its parent species. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella × variifurca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Pilosella × variifurca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Pilosella × variifurca is classified as a hybrid species within the Asteraceae family. It is a cross between Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella caespitosa. As a result of its hybrid nature, Pilosella × variifurca has unique characteristics that distinguish it from its parent species. The species is further categorized within the subgenus Eunomia, which includes several other similar hybrid species. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships between different Pilosella species.
uses: Pilosella × variifurca is a hybrid species with various reported traditional uses. However, I could not find any reliable information about its specific medicinal uses. benefits: Pilosella × variifurca is a type of flowering plant that has been studied for its potential health benefits. Research suggests that extracts from this plant may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, some studies indicate that Pilosella × variifurca may have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which could help protect against cell damage and infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of this plant. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
properties: Pilosella × variifurca, a hybrid of the Pilosella species, is known for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant has been studied for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. constituents: Pilosella × variifurca is known to contain sesquiterpene lactones, which are a class of compounds found in many plants of the Asteraceae family. These sesquiterpene lactones have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The specific composition of Pilosella × variifurca may also include flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributing to its potential medicinal uses. preparations: Pilosella × variifurca is a hybrid plant species used in traditional herbal medicine, primarily in Europe.
